meta.title: Canonical Example: The Act Face (The Operators)

current_revision.content:
Each position is defined here by what the embedding space actually finds most geometrically characteristic of it — not by theoretical description. Examples are the top-ranked clauses by discrimination margin: how far a clause sits from its nearest competing position. Higher margin = more unambiguously in this region of semantic space and away from all others. All 27 positions derive from the same corpus of ~19,764 classified clauses across 41 languages.

The Act face is Mode × Domain — the crossing of how a transformation is structured with what level of reality it operates on.

Mode axis (Q1)

Domain axis (Q2)

DIFFERENTIATING — separating, bounding, distinguishing

EXISTENCE — being, presence, instantiation

RELATING — connecting, holding in relation, co-presencing

STRUCTURE — organization, relation, system

GENERATING — producing, integrating, transforming

SIGNIFICANCE — meaning, interpretation, value

The nine operators arise from crossing these two axes. The cell address is Mode × Domain.

NUL — DIFFERENTIATING × EXISTENCE

Recognize absence. Something that could be present is not. The operation marks an empty slot — before difference can register, the absence of something must first be acknowledged.

Top 25 exemplars by discrimination margin:

#

Language

Δ

Original

Translation

1

Italian

0.098

"La SpaceAfter=Norajina non esiste più".

"The SpaceAfter=Norajina no longer exists."

2

Spanish

0.094

Como no existen asentamientos permanentes, también es la isla deshabitada mayor del hemisferio norte...

Since no permanent settlements exist, it is also the largest uninhabited island in the northern hemisphere...

3

Ancient Greek

0.089

οἱ δὲ παραγενόμενοι ὑπηρέται οὐχ εὗρον αὐτοὺς ἐν τῇ φυλακῇ

But the officers who came did not find them in the prison.

4

Ancient Greek

0.088

τῆς δὲ Σπάρτης Ἀναξανδρίδης μὲν ὁ Λέοντος οὐκέτι περιεὼν ἐβασίλευε ἀλλὰ ἐτετελευτήκεε

Of Sparta, Anaxandrides son of Leon was no longer alive to reign but had died.

5

Ancient Greek

0.085

ὃς ἀνῃρέθη καὶ πάντες ὅσοι ἐπείθοντο αὐτῷ διελύθησαν καὶ ἐγένοντο εἰς οὐδέν

He was killed and all who had obeyed him were scattered and came to nothing.

6

0.076

7

0.075

今年はある事情により姿を見せなかったようだ。

It appears that this year, due to certain circumstances, they did not appear.

8

There is no longer an empire of evil to fight.

9

0.074

10

Ancient Greek

0.074

When he arrived in Milesian territory, he neither destroyed nor burned the farmhouses.

11

Italian

0.071

Nell'incidente non sono rimaste coinvolte persone.

No people were involved in the incident.

12

0.068

omnis arbor quae non facit fructum bonum exciditur et in ignem mittitur

15

16

Spanish

0.064

No se respetan las citas y las anulan a su antojo.

17

Italian

0.064

He is leaving, like Lewis, without participating (official: sails Wednesday).

18

Basque

0.064

Last year neither Jan Ullrich nor Marco Pantani were present.

19

Danish

0.063

Der var ingen penge til spillerlønninger eller præmier.

There was no money for player wages or prizes.

20

Hungarian

0.062

A másik kocsinak már a nyomai sem látszanak.

Not even the traces of the other car are visible anymore.

21

Spanish

0.062

Las habitaciones tenian las paredes desconchadas, no funcionaba el aire acondicionado en ninguna de las habitaciones...

The rooms had peeling walls, the air conditioning didn't work in any of the rooms...

22

Latin

0.061

omnis ergo arbor quae non facit fructum bonum exciditur et in ignem mittitur

Therefore every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.

23

Spanish

0.060

Ninguna de las estructuras federales puede influir en esta elección, ya que no existe ninguna dependencia formal.

None of the federal structures can influence this election, since no formal dependency exists.

24

Norwegian

0.060

Espen hadde ikke vært å se i det hun kom ut fra Exodus.

Espen had not been seen when she came out of Exodus.

25

Latin

0.058

omnis plantatio quam non plantavit Pater meus caelestis eradicabitur

Every plant that my heavenly Father has not planted will be uprooted.

Language weights: Ancient Greek (4), Latin (3), Spanish (4), Italian (3), Indonesian, Portuguese, Catalan, Bulgarian, Romanian, Arabic, Japanese, Basque, Danish, Hungarian, Norwegian.

Term/semantic pattern: Negated existential verbs dominate — non esiste, no existen, οὐχ εὗρον, tidak ada, não há, no s-a regăsit, ولا توجد, hadn't been seen, no money, no traces, no longer exists. The corpus finds NUL most cleanly in clauses whose main semantic content is the non-presence of something expected. Ancient Greek and Latin carry it with particular precision: both languages have strong grammatical resources for marking absence as a positive fact rather than a failed search.

SIG — RELATING × EXISTENCE

Register difference. Something becomes noticeable — a distinction emerges at the level of existence itself. The minimal act of marking that something is here as opposed to not-here.

Top 25 exemplars by discrimination margin:

#

Language

Δ

Original

Translation

1

German

0.126

Musste auch nich lange auf einen Termin warten und Wartezeiten waren meistens auch kurz.

Didn't have to wait long for an appointment and waiting times were mostly short too.

2

0.109

Pizza gibt es dort, auch wenn die Wartezeiten manchmal etwas lang werden können.

Pizza is available there, even though the waiting times can sometimes get a bit long.

3

0.105

Wenn man etwas braucht kann man das auch noch mitnehmen.

When you need something you can also take it with you.

0.105

5

0.104

7

10

0.094

ich bin recht oft in Hotels unterwegs.

11

German

0.094

12

German

0.093

Ich war schon bei vielen Steak Restaurants, vor gut 2 Tagen habe ich dieses Steakhaus gefunden.

13

German

0.093

14

0.091

0.090

Inzwischen lebt sie wesentlich selbstbestimmter und froher auch wenn es lange dauert bis sie regeneriert.

She now lives much more self-determined and happily, even though it takes a long time for her to recover.

16

German

0.089

Sind zum Kurzurlaub in Duhnen gewesen und fanden aus Zufall ganz am Ende der Strasse im Hafen dieses tolle Restaurant.

We went on a short holiday to Duhnen and by chance found this wonderful restaurant at the very end of the street in the harbor.

17

German

0.086

Habe heute online bestellt, ich war erstaunt wie schnell das Essen da war.

I ordered online today, I was amazed at how quickly the food arrived.

18

German

0.086

Wir hatten Hunger und gingen in dieses Restaurant.

We were hungry and went into this restaurant.

19

German

0.086

Bei meinem Abstecher in die alte Heimat bin ich in der wunderschönen Pension von Familie Klaua in Bernsdorf untergekommen.

On my visit to my old hometown I stayed at the wonderful guesthouse of the Klaua family in Bernsdorf.

20

German

0.085

Trotz Termin musste ich 60 Minuten warten.

Despite having an appointment, I had to wait 60 minutes.

21

German

0.083

Lange hat es gedauert bis es auch hier die skandinavischen Modemarken erhältlich sind.

It took a long time for Scandinavian fashion brands to become available here too.

22

German

0.083

Danke... die Blumen sind genau zur richtigen Zeit eingetroffen... ;-)

Thank you... the flowers arrived at exactly the right time... ;-)

23

German

0.080

Ich bin seit fast 5 Jahren auf der Suche nach jemanden gewesen, der mich nicht übers Ohr hauen wollte.

I've been searching for almost 5 years for someone who didn't want to rip me off.

24

German

0.080

Bereits letzte Woche war ich bei einer anderen HNO-Ärztin, wegen meiner Beschwerden.

Last week I had already been to another ENT specialist about my complaints.

25

German

0.078

Finden kann man wirklich alles was man sucht.

You can really find everything you're looking for.

Language weights: German (25/25). ⚠ Single-language concentration — all top exemplars from German service review corpus. This may be a corpus artifact; the concentration in one register and one language means SIG's embedding position may be shaped by a specific corpus cluster rather than a universal pattern.

Term/semantic pattern: Every clause registers a state that has come to the classifier's notice — waiting times are short, food arrived quickly, appointment was or wasn't available, something was or wasn't found. The grammatical structure is consistently observational present-tense noticing: something is registering as present, available, or remarkable in its absence. The SIG position in the embedding space appears to be captured by clauses that register small facts about availability and presence without any further transformation.

INS — GENERATING × EXISTENCE

Create instance. Something new comes into existence as a discrete event. The operation instantiates — a new concrete thing that was not there before now is.

Language

Δ

Original

Translation

0.070

0.069

Language weights: Hebrew (6), Hungarian (3), Tamil (2), Urdu (3), Korean (2), Japanese, Dutch, Chinese, Romanian, Danish, Arabic, English.

Term/semantic pattern: Sudden discrete events involving casualties, destruction, or creation. The INS position is consistently occupied by clauses where something new enters existence as a fait accompli — deaths, attacks, outbreaks, collapses, explosions. The grammar is past-tense completion; the semantic content is irreversible instantiation. The corpus finds INS most cleanly in conflict and disaster reporting across South Asian, Middle Eastern, and East Asian language corpora.

SEG — DIFFERENTIATING × STRUCTURE

Draw boundary. Something is partitioned, divided, set apart from the rest. The operation separates elements within a structure — not recognizing absence (NUL) but actively drawing a line.

Term/semantic pattern: Statistical distributions, partitions, separations, divisions. The SEG position is dominated by clauses that draw boundaries within a structure — percentages allocated, populations distributed, assets divided, parties split. Swedish analytical prose is the strongest carrier, with its tradition of distributional comparison and systematic partitioning.

Connect across boundary. Two or more things are held in relation — co-present, associated, participating together in something. The operation maintains a connection across a structural boundary.

Term/semantic pattern: Two or more specific agents co-present in a structured context. The CON position is densely populated by diplomatic and photographic description — two presidents attending a forum together, flags taking their place alongside others, a child held by a man while a woman stands beside. The South Slavic languages (Croatian, Serbian) and Polish dominate, sharing a grammar that naturally encodes participation, co-attendance, and structured co-presence with minimal interpretive loading.

SYN — GENERATING × STRUCTURE

Merge into emergent whole. Parts integrate into something that exceeds their sum — a new structural unity is produced. The operation synthesizes rather than connects.

The EVA position is occupied by clauses that revise what something means within an existing frame — "it is not race," "one cannot say that," "nothing can be proven," "what he claims is far from true." Russian opinion journalism dominates. The grammatical pattern is consistently disputative or corrective: a prior characterization is being changed, not the frame that produces it.

Language weights: Slovak (8), Vietnamese (5), Turkish (4), Czech (2), Ancient Greek, Wolof, Danish, Finnish, Chinese, Bulgarian.

Term/semantic pattern: Reported speech and social navigation — someone saying something that simultaneously concedes and maintains their position. "All right," agreed Fil. "Everyone's time is important," Kerem said. "I also feel like I've met you somewhere." The DEF position is saturated with indirect speech, diplomatic politeness, and the language of characters holding multiple social positions at once. Slovak fiction, Vietnamese conversation, and Turkish reported speech carry this most distinctly.

REC — GENERATING × SIGNIFICANCE

Change the frame itself. The interpretive structure through which something is understood undergoes transformation. Not revising a value within a frame (EVA) but reconstituting the frame.

Language weights: German (7), French (5), Italian (4), Dutch (2), Arabic, Portuguese, Indonesian, Norwegian, Japanese, Spanish, Chinese, Hungarian.

Term/semantic pattern: Revised total assessments — first impressions reversed, frames reconstituted, people or situations re-characterized at the level of their identity. "My impression has completely reversed." "The world will see him as a swindler." "Covers himself in ridicule." "Rhetoric became a political weapon." REC clauses re-categorize rather than revise — they don't say "this was worse than expected" but "this was a different kind of thing entirely." German and French judgmental prose carry it most distinctly.
